name: Ginger Beer Starter
recipeIngredient: Foam from 12-ounce can of chilled beer
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the potatoes in water until tender.
2. Drain and add more water if needed to make 3 full cups.
3. Pass through a food mill or sieve.
4. Scald corn meal in 1 cup potato water until boiling, stirring constantly.
5. Combine sieved potatoes, 2 cups potato water, scalded corn meal, sugar, and salt in a bowl (avoid metal or plastic).
6. Let cool to warm.
7. Slowly pour cold beer into the mixture; skim off foam.
8. Stir into the bowl mix; cover tightly.
9. Set in a warm place (80-85°F) until foamy throughout.
10. Pour into a large glass container, refrigerate at 38°F.
11. Let ripen for about 3 days until clear liquid rises to the top.
12. Stir before using any portion.
13. When reduced to about 1 cup, repeat with remaining old starter and new beer foam.
Using Beer Starter: Replace beer foam with half a cup of English Ginger Beer from supermarkets.
